settings.headline:
CRUNCH
DIFFERENT.
settings.body:
We started Inhaus Khakhra with one complaint: why does “healthy” always have to taste like punishment?

Khakhra’s been a staple in Indian kitchens forever — thin, roasted, unapologetically crunchy. We didn’t reinvent it. We just refused to make it boring.

Every coin is stone-baked, not fried, in small batches, then loaded with flavours that actually taste like something — from the sour hit of Pani Puri to the loud heat of Schezwan. No palm oil. No shortcuts. No pretending a snack has to be dull to be good for you.

Small. Round. Addictive. That’s the whole pitch.
